big_picture: The government waived duties on kerosene and diesel to shield consumers from rising crude prices amid the West Asia conflict. The Rajya Sabha passed the Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Amendment Bill, 2026, following clearance by the Lok Sabha. The Lok Sabha passed the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC) Amendment Bill to expedite insolvency resolutions and curb backlogs. The Opposition staged a walkout in the Rajya Sabha demanding that the CAPF Bill be sent to a select panel of Parliament. Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman did not comment on the amendment to the Foreign Contribution Regulation Act (FCRA). The Transgender Persons Amendment Bill and the question of identity has sparked debate over who gets to define a trans life.

story_clusters.headline: Gas crunch hits Firozabad's glass industry as West Asia conflict continues
story_clusters.summary: The ongoing conflict in West Asia has disrupted gas supplies, impacting India’s glass industry in Firozabad and prompting concerns over economic growth. Prime Minister Narendra Modi has directed states to monitor supply chains and prevent price manipulation amid the crisis, while also engaging with Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman in a second call since the conflict began. Economic analysts, such as EY, warn that the conflict could reduce India’s FY27 GDP growth forecast by up to 1 percentage point.

story_clusters.summary: The Indian Rupee strengthened against the US dollar, rising above 93 for the first time in nearly two weeks after the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) implemented measures to curb forex speculation. Reports indicate the RBI's actions disrupted the $149 billion-a-day offshore rupee market, with the currency recording its sharpest rise in over 12 years. The Rupee's gains were reported at 2% by ET Markets and 1.6% by Mint Markets.

perspectives.title: Rahul Gandhi Calls Himanta Biswa Sarma Most Corrupt CM of India
perspectives.unique_angle: Rahul Gandhi Calls Himanta Biswa Sarma Most Corrupt CM of India Rahul said he converted Assam into a "land ATM" for corporates

story_clusters.summary: Rahul Gandhi has accused Himanta Biswa Sarma of being the "most corrupt Chief Minister in India," according to reports from ThePrint India and Deccan Chronicle. Gandhi also alleged that Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Home Minister Amit Shah are involved in running a "land ATM" in Assam.

perspectives.unique_angle: Anil Ambani faces fresh CBI case over LIC fraud allegations CBI files new case against Anil Ambani and Reliance Communications for allegedly defrauding LIC of over 4500 crore via NCDs, funds allegedly

story_clusters.summary: The Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) has filed a fresh First Information Report (FIR) against Anil Ambani and Reliance Communications (RCom) based on a complaint from Life Insurance Corporation (LIC) alleging a financial loss of ₹3,700 crore. The case pertains to alleged fraud related to the LIC complaint.

perspectives.unique_angle: ‘Beware of Congress, it is planning to bring a dangerous law in Assam’: PM Modi The PM alleged that the Congress was preparing to introduce a law that would unfairly target the majority community

story_clusters.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has accused the Congress party of planning to introduce a law in Assam that he claims would protect infiltrators. He warned voters to be cautious of Congress ahead of the 2026 Assam Assembly Elections.

perspectives.unique_angle: Govt defers debate on FCRA bill after stir by Oppn, row in Kerala The government postponed the Foreign Contribution (Regulation) Amendment Bill amid Opposition protests, fearing it could target Christ

story_clusters.summary: The FCRA Amendment Bill, 2026, has drawn criticism for its "Draconian Provisions," with sources highlighting concerns over its potential shift "From Regulation To Expropriation." The government deferred debate on the bill following opposition from political parties and protests in Kerala.

perspectives.title: OMCs stare at significant losses on domestic LPG sales; govt likely to take a fiscal hit on compensating OMCs
perspectives.unique_angle: OMCs stare at significant losses on domestic LPG sales; govt likely to take a fiscal hit on compensating OMCs India faces major fiscal strain as war in West Asia drives LPG under recoveries of 380 rup

story_clusters.summary: The Cabinet Committee on Security was briefed on issues related to LPG/LNG and fertilisers, with Prime Minister Modi directing all necessary steps to address the situation. Reports indicate that oil marketing companies (OMCs) face significant losses on domestic LPG sales, and the government may incur a fiscal burden in compensating them.

Civil Liberties.summary:
The Transgender Persons Amendment Bill, 2026, was passed by the Parliament following clearance by the Rajya Sabha, deleting the provision surrounding the right to a self-perceived gender identity.

Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Stalin alleged that the previous AIADMK government failed to effectively act on crimes against women.

Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Stalin stated that an amendment to the FCRA is a direct attack on minority institutions, despite the government stepping back for now due to opposition protests.
Civil Liberties.top_article_title: The Transgender Persons Amendment Bill, 2026, and its Discontents

Politics.summary:
The Lok Sabha passed a bill recognizing Amaravati as the sole capital of Andhra Pradesh, which will prevent any future attempts to alter this decision.

The Rajya Sabha passed the IBC Amendment Bill with a voice vote, following its approval by the Lok Sabha on March 30, to expedite insolvency resolutions and curb backlogs.

Parliament has approved changes to the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code, which aim to speed up resolutions and improve the financial system, with Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman highlighting the IBC's success in asset recovery.
Politics.top_article_title: Parliament Clears IBC Amendment Bill to Expedite Insolvency Resolutions and Curb Backlogs

Legal.summary:
The Supreme Court set aside the Punjab & Haryana High Court's order directing voter examination and fingerprint analysis in a double voting case, as no such plea was made before the Election Tribunal.

The Delhi High Court clarified that the results of Bar Council of Delhi (BCD) elections 2026 shall be declared only after the reconciliation of votes and ballots is concluded.

The Punjab and Haryana High Court asked two lawyers to inform since when the Punjab government is charging an Rs. 80 service charge for downloading FIR copies on the 'Punjab Police Sanjh Portal'.
Legal.top_article_title: Election Petition Must Be Decided On Materials On Record, Can't Be Remanded To Fill Evidentiary Gaps : Supreme Court

Finance.summary:
Economists expect the RBI to focus on supporting economic growth while managing inflation risks, with potential adjustments to liquidity conditions based on evolving conditions.

The RBI may keep the repo rate unchanged due to persisting inflation risks, with the central bank closely monitoring the situation before taking further policy action.

Businesses in India have 191 critical compliance deadlines across various laws, including FEMA, GST, Income Tax, and the Companies Act, in April 2026.
Finance.top_article_title: What will RBI do on April 8: We asked four of the top economists

Energy.summary:
Commercial LPG prices in Delhi, India have increased by Rs 195.5, with a 19-kg cylinder now costing Rs 2,078.5.

The Indian government's reinstatement of export duties may lead to a margin squeeze for refiners due to a dip in refining margins and a mandate to produce more LPG.

In India, urban households predominantly use liquefied petroleum gas for cooking, while rural households, particularly in poorer states, rely heavily on firewood.
Energy.top_article_title: Commercial LPG prices increased by Rs 195.5 amid Iran war

Governance.summary:
The Deputy Commissioner of India's Legal Metrology Department advised buyers to be cautious when purchasing gold and to insist on separate weighing for precious stones before they are inserted into gold ornaments.

Mubarik Gul, a leader from the National Conference, predicted the abrogation of Article 370 and the division of the state, stating "He stood up, banged the table and said it will go, state will be split."

The company X told the Centre that blocking 12 of its accounts is 'excessive and disproportionate', citing concerns that the permanent suspension of the accounts in India would prevent the profile holder from using the social media platform in the country.
Governance.top_article_title: X tells Centre orders to block 12 accounts are ‘excessive and disproportionate’

Defence.summary:
Indian Navy Chief Admiral Dinesh K Tripathi revealed the Navy was moments from striking Pakistan by sea during Operation Sindoor, which targeted terrorist bases launched after the Pahalgam terror attack. 

A devastating incident in the city of Minab, Iran, where a U.S. military strike allegedly hit the Shajareh Tayyebeh school, resulting in the deaths of more than 168 civilians, has become a defining test for International Humanitarian Law. 

The Delhi High Court has issued a notice to the CBI, CAG, and Centre over corruption allegations in the Indian Army, seeking responses on a plea for a court-monitored probe into alleged procurement corruption.
Defence.top_article_title: 'Minutes away from striking Pakistan from sea': Navy chief Dinesh K Tripathi reveals key Operation Sindoor moment

Trade.summary: India faced significant challenges in 2025 due to President Trump’s tariffs, forcing New Delhi to diversify exports and strengthen domestic industries. India’s defence exports reached a record ₹38,424 crore, with Rajnath Singh attributing the growth to global confidence in India’s indigenous manufacturing. India’s manufacturing PMI slowed to 53.9 in March due to rising costs, competition, and Middle East conflicts, despite strong export demand.
Trade.top_article_title: One battle after another: How India fared in the year of Trump tariffs

Health.summary: India is set to introduce the dengue vaccine Qdenga soon, with doctors Rashmi Khadapkar and Neha Rastogi explaining its expected impact and usage. Indian pharmaceutical firms are expanding the anti-obesity drug market with low-cost generics following patent expiry, including existing options and upcoming treatments. The Delhi High Court rejected a prisoner’s transfer plea, directing a second medical opinion from Apollo Hospital’s Endocrinology and Neurology Departments.
Health.top_article_title: India To Get Dengue Vaccine Qdenga Soon, Doctors Share What To Expect

Technology.summary: ANSR launched a Healthcare GCC Accelerator powered by advanced AI capabilities. T9L Venture Studio backed Forsyt, a 'Revenue OS' startup, with a strategic investment. WhatsApp stated that an Italian surveillance company tricked around 200 users into downloading spyware.
Technology.top_article_title: ANSR Launches Healthcare GCC Accelerator Powered by Advanced AI Capabilities

Infrastructure.summary: The road ministry will compensate contractors monthly for price hikes and volatility in commodity prices, shortening the price adjustment period to one month for three months. The Railways upgraded the 161-year-old Yamuna Bridge in Uttar Pradesh to improve train services on key routes. PM Modi launched the Noida International Airport in Jewar, detailing connected cities, airlines, and key details.
Infrastructure.top_article_title: Road ministry to compensate for price hike and volatility in commodity prices to contractors

Foreign.summary:
Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov will visit New Delhi in mid-May for a BRICS meeting.

Russian oil reaches India via two primary routes—from Baltic ports to Gujarat through ship-to-ship transfers, sometimes exploiting price cap loopholes.

Bangladesh’s Foreign Minister will undertake a three-nation tour in April, including stops in India, Mauritius, and Pakistan, marking the first visit by a Bangladeshi foreign minister to New Delhi since former Prime Minister Hasina’s ouster.
Foreign.top_article_title: Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov to visit Delhi mid May for BRICS meet

Telecom.summary:
India's telecom subscriber base reached 1.32 billion in February, adding 7.31 million subscribers at a growth rate of 0.56%, driven by surging broadband adoption.

TRAI released Telecom Subscription Data as of February 2026.

Nokia’s report projects India’s 5G subscriber base to surpass 1 billion by 2031, noting average monthly mobile data consumption per user rose to over 31 GB in 2025 from 27.5 GB in 2024.
Telecom.top_article_title: India's telecom subscriber base hits 1.32 billion as broadband adoption surges in February

Agriculture.summary: The Centre is cracking down on fertiliser black-marketing while reviewing the Minimum Support Price (MSP) procurement process. Odisha has extended Kharif paddy procurement by 7 days to safeguard farmers’ interests, having already procured around 77 lakh metric tonnes. India's dairy sector leaders in New Delhi emphasized the need for a climate-resilient and future-ready ecosystem to secure sustainable rural livelihoods.
Agriculture.top_article_title: Centre cracks down on fertiliser black-marketing, reviews MSP procurement

Labour.summary: The protest against India’s four new labour codes coincides with workers facing economic hardship due to the US-Israel war on Iran. A Business Standard article highlights how companies risk having compliance staff make up 25% of their workforce if they don’t adapt to regulatory demands. Oracle laid off 30,000 employees despite strong revenue growth, as reported by Business Standard.
Labour.top_article_title: Panipat’s battle for labour rights shows why workers across India are going on strike today

Urban.summary: Itanagar’s municipal body ordered eateries to remove 'chicken', 'beef', 'pork', and 'mutton' from their names for being inappropriate and inconsistent with public decency norms. NBCC (India) secured a $75 million affordable housing project mandate in Seychelles, expanding its domestic and international order pipeline. Vodafone Idea, Ola Electric Mobility, YES Bank, Eternal, Tata Silver Exchange Traded Fund, Tata Gold Exchange Traded Fund, and Nippon India ETF Gold Bees were among the most actively traded stocks on the NSE today.
Urban.top_article_title: Itanagar eateries told to drop ‘chicken’, ‘beef’, ‘pork’, ‘mutton’ from names

Environment.summary: India has set a new target to reduce its emissions intensity under its updated Paris Agreement pledge. 53 gharials were released into the river at Kuno National Park in Madhya Pradesh as part of conservation efforts for the species. Carbon Brief’s DeBriefed addressed misconceptions about the North Sea while covering India’s climate plan and the IPCC’s stance on Indigenous knowledge.
Environment.top_article_title: Q&A: What does India’s new Paris Agreement pledge mean for climate action?

DeepTech.summary:
Happiest Minds Technologies is preparing to launch its first agentic AI service delivery platform, developed over 18 months, as a key differentiator for the ₹5,800-crore company.

Anthropic’s AI coding tool, Claude Code, exposed its full source code due to a packaging error, though no user data or core AI systems were compromised.

Business Standard reports that rising AI adoption is driving renewed emphasis on humanities and critical thinking.
DeepTech.top_article_title: Happiest Minds may launch its first Agentic AI for engineering soon

Welfare.summary: In Madhya Pradesh, some households have reverted to using dung-cake stoves despite previously benefiting from the Ujjwala scheme for smoke-free kitchens. The interest rate on deposits under the Sukanya Samriddhi Scheme remains unchanged at 8.2%, while the rate on a three-year term deposit stays at 7.1%.
Welfare.top_article_title: Madhya Pradesh: Ujjwala gave them smoke-free kitchens, but now, dung-cake stoves are back

Education.summary: Jammu University removed references to Muslim thinkers from its syllabus, erasing key figures from Indian and South Asian history. The CBSE introduced a new curriculum on Computational Thinking and AI for Classes 3 to 8 to improve AI readiness and digital citizenship among students. Karnataka’s draft policy aims to reduce student screen time, prioritizing mental health and cyber safety by proposing limits and banning social media for under-16s.
Education.top_article_title: What Jammu University gets wrong about removing ‘Muslim’ thinkers from the syllabus

Fintech.summary: In March, UPI transactions in India increased by 11% month-over-month to reach 22.64 billion.
Fintech.top_article_title: UPI Transactions Jump 11% MoM To 22.64 Bn In March

Data Privacy.summary: Neelam Dhawan has been appointed as the new Chairperson of the Data Security Council of India, succeeding Promod Bhasin. Dhawan will collaborate with industry and government to enhance India's cybersecurity and data privacy efforts. The initiative aims to position India as a global leader in cybersecurity.
Data Privacy.top_article_title: Nasscom's Data Security Council appoints Neelam Dhawan as chairperson

articles.title: Supreme Court Weekly Digest February 12 - 20, 2026
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has ruled that a Compulsory License for Undertaking (CLU) cannot be validated retrospectively if it was issued without statutory backing. This decision has significant implications for the pharmaceutical industry in India. The court held that when a statute prescribes a particular manner for doing an act, it must be done in that manner and no other.

articles.title: Parliament passes amendments to IBC to quicken resolution process, reduce backlog
articles.summary: Parliament has approved changes to the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code. These amendments aim to speed up resolutions and improve the financial system. The Rajya Sabha passed the bill after the Lok Sabha's approval. Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman highlighted the IBC's success in asset recovery. The changes include provisions to reduce the time for admitting insolvency applications.

articles.title: BREAKING| Supreme Court Slams West Bengal Officials Over  Attack On Judges During SIR Duty, Directs Use Of Central Forces
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has directed the Election Commission of India to use central forces and entrust the investigation of an attack on judges during their duty to either the CBI or the NIA. This decision aims to ensure the safety and security of judges in West Bengal. The Court's action is a significant step in addressing the concerns of the judiciary and upholding the rule of law.
articles.raw_summary: The Court directed the ECI to entrust the investigation to either the CBI or the NIA.

articles.title: Proviso To Order VI Rule 17 CPC Not Strictly Applicable To Amendment Of Grounds In Revision Or Appellate Proceedings: Allahabad High Court
articles.summary: The Allahabad High Court has held that the Proviso to Order VI Rule 17 CPC, which bars amendment in a suit after commencement of trial, is not strictly applicable to amendment of grounds in revision or appellate proceedings. Order VI Rule 17 provides for amendment to pleadings. The proviso to Rule 17 provides that no amendment can be allowed after the trial has commenced, unless the Court concludes that such grounds could not have been raised even with due diligence before commencement of trial.

The quantum of power should be firm power for the entire duration of the contract.

articles.title: Lok Sabha passes Jan Vishwas amendment bill
articles.summary: The Lok Sabha has approved the Jan Vishwas Bill. This legislation aims to simplify business and daily life by decriminalising minor offences. It will amend 784 provisions across 79 Central Acts. The bill seeks to remove outdated rules and rationalise over 1,000 offences. This move is expected to benefit individuals and MSMEs by improving the regulatory environment.

articles.title: India 10-year bond yield cross 7% amid surge in crude oil prices; likely to remain elevated

articles.title: Judicial officers will decide 60L Bengal claims by Apr 7, 47.3L completed: Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has directed that all deleted voter claims in West Bengal must be decided by April 7, ahead of the assembly elections. Judicial officers have been tasked with ensuring independence from political interference. Appellate tribunals have been formed to hear objections. Further hearings are scheduled for April 6.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court has been informed that all deleted voter claims in West Bengal will be decided by April 7. This addresses concerns about the upcoming assembly elections. Appellate tribunals have been formed to hear any objections. The court emphasized that judicial officers must remain independent and free from political interference. Further hearings are scheduled for April 6.

articles.title: Maharashtra Forms State Authority To Restore 54 Polluted Rivers
articles.summary: The Maharashtra government, led by Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis, has decided to establish a state authority to restore 54 polluted rivers. This decision was taken during a cabinet meeting at Mantralaya. The move aims to address severe water pollution issues affecting multiple rivers in the state. The new authority will be tasked with implementing restoration measures to improve river health and water quality.
articles.raw_summary: The decision was taken at a cabinet meeting chaired by Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis at Mantralaya.

articles.title: India's sugar production rises 9% to 272.31 lakh tons till March 31: ISMA
articles.summary: India's sugar production saw a 9 percent rise in the 2025-26 season. However, fewer sugar mills are operational this year. The industry is urging for an early increase in the Minimum Selling Price to manage rising costs. Accelerated ethanol blending is also proposed to boost energy security and support the sector. Disruptions in LPG supply have impacted sugar consumption.

articles.title: India's March palm oil imports fall to 3-month low as prices surge
articles.summary: March brought a sharp turn for India's edible oil import scene, with palm oil imports witnessing a striking 19% decrease, marking a three-month low. India is transforming how it transacts.

articles.title: Indirect tax mop-up tops FY26 target, but pan masala cess misses the mark
articles.summary: India's indirect tax collections for fiscal year 2025-26 have surpassed the government's target. Customs, excise, and GST revenues showed strong performance. articles.title: India factory activity cooled in March with oil costs rising amid Middle East turmoil: PMI
articles.summary: India's manufacturing sector saw its slowest growth in almost four years during March. The Middle East conflict caused supply chain issues and reduced demand. Input costs rose significantly, impacting manufacturers. Despite challenges, export orders increased. Firms maintained employment growth and remained optimistic about future expansion and agricultural strength.

articles.title: Centre pushes states on fertiliser black-marketing, MSP procurement; 9.25 crore farmer IDs created
articles.summary: Union Agriculture Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan urged states to achieve 100% farmer registration for schemes within six months, emphasizing the need to curb fertilizer hoarding and black-marketing. articles.title: Centre waives duties on key petrochemical products amid supply disruptions due to Iran war
articles.summary: India has waived import duties on crucial chemicals like ammonium nitrate, methanol, and PVC for three months, starting April 2nd. This move aims to ease supply chains and potentially lower costs for various industries. The government has also exempted ammonium nitrate from the Agriculture Infrastructure and Development Cess during this period, offering further relief.

articles.title: Rising yields amid Middle East crisis trigger Rs 17,689 cr FPI outflow from FAR G-Sec
articles.summary: Global investors have withdrawn nearly Rs 17,700 crore from Indian government bonds since the Middle East conflict began, driven by rising risk aversion and inflation fears from soaring crude oil prices. This outflow has pushed domestic bond yields higher, with the benchmark 10-year bond exceeding 7% for the first time in over 20 months, making existing holdings less attractive.

articles.title: As GLP-1 generics flood market, govt intensifies surveillance
articles.summary: The Indian government is intensifying surveillance on the market for GLP-1 generics, a class of drugs used for conditions like diabetes and obesity. This move comes as generic versions of these drugs flood the market, prompting regulatory scrutiny. The action reflects concerns over quality, pricing, or potential misuse of these medications. The government's focus suggests a proactive approach to ensuring drug safety and affordability in India.

articles.title: CBI files fresh FIR against Anil, RCom on LIC complaint alleging 3.7k cr loss
articles.summary: The CBI has filed a new case against Anil Ambani and Reliance Communications, along with bank officials, following an LIC complaint. Allegations suggest LIC was misled into investing Rs 4,500 crore in Non-Convertible Debentures based on false financial representations. A forensic audit revealed alleged misuse of funds, including siphoning through subsidiaries and fictitious transactions, causing a Rs 3,750 crore loss.

articles.title: Ravi Varma’s ‘Yashoda & Krishna’ fetches record-shattering Rs 167 crore
articles.summary: Industrialist Cyrus S Poonawalla has set a new record for modern Indian art, acquiring Raja Ravi Varma's 'Yashoda and Krishna' for Rs 167.2 crore. This landmark sale, which more than doubled its estimate, highlights the enduring cultural significance of Varma's work. Poonawalla plans to make the national treasure available for public viewing.

articles.title: Despite revamp & AMC, Beijing embassy 'uninhabitable': CAG
articles.summary: The Comptroller and Auditor General (CAG) of India has reported that residential units at the Chinese embassy in Beijing are uninhabitable, with many vacant or dilapidated. Officials are reportedly living in rented accommodations outside the embassy premises. The CAG's findings suggest systemic failures in the maintenance and management of diplomatic housing by the Indian government. This raises concerns about governance and administrative oversight in handling foreign missions' infrastructure.
articles.raw_summary: 'Residential Units Vacant Or Dilapidated, Officials Living On Rent Outside'

articles.title: March PMI snapshot: India’s manufacturing growth slows to 53.9 as rising cost pressures weigh on momentum
articles.summary: India's manufacturing sector experienced a slowdown in March, with growth moderating due to rising costs, intense competition, and global uncertainties like the Middle East conflict. While new orders and output saw slower expansion, employment surged, and export demand remained robust, indicating a mixed but resilient industrial landscape.

While new orders and output saw slower expansion, employment surged, and export demand remained robust, indicating a mixed but resilient industrial landscape.

articles.title: Rupee climbs 151 paise to reach 93.19 against US dollar
articles.summary: The Indian rupee rebounded strongly, gaining 151 paise to 93.19 against the US dollar after the Reserve Bank capped banks' net open positions. Despite this regulatory intervention, underlying pressures from foreign fund outflows, a firming dollar, and high crude oil prices persist, impacting equity markets as well.

articles.title: Centre grants full customs duty exemption on critical petrochemical products amid Middle East crisis: Check list
articles.summary: The Indian government has announced a full exemption on customs duties for critical petrochemical products until June 30, amid disruptions in Middle East energy supplies. This move aims to ease supply chain pressures, reduce costs for downstream industries, and ensure the availability of essential inputs for manufacturing. The exemption applies to key petrochemical items, as notified by the government.

This temporary measure aims to alleviate supply chain challenges, reduce cost pressures on downstream industries, and ensure the continued availability of essential inputs for various manufacturing sectors.

articles.title: New I-T Act marks new chapter in India's tax administration, step towards Viksit Bharat: CBDT
articles.summary: India has a new Income Tax Act, 2025, effective April 1, 2026. This replaces the old 1961 Act, making tax rules simpler and easier to follow. The e-filing portal will support both old and new acts during the transition. Assessments for past years will follow the old Act. Advance tax payments from June 2026 will use the new Act.

articles.title: Why RBI is clamping down on FX arbitrage
articles.summary: India's central bank has deployed crisis-era measures to stabilize the rupee, which hit an all-time low due to soaring oil prices and massive foreign investor outflows. The Reserve Bank of India is curbing speculative arbitrage trades that amplified currency pressure. These actions aim to reduce dollar demand and support the rupee, though they may increase hedging costs for overseas investors.

articles.title: In Departmental Enquiry, Document Not Admitted By Employee Must Be Proved Through Witness : Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that an employee cannot be dismissed based solely on unproven documentary evidence if the charges are denied. Employers must present witnesses to substantiate such documents, allowing the employee to cross-examine them. This decision impacts departmental enquiries and employment termination processes across India.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court on Wednesday (April 1) has observed that when the employee doesn't admit the charges labelled against him, he cannot be dismissed from the services based on an employer's unproven documentary evidence. articles.title: New 4-dustbin waste segregation rule kicks in: Check what green, red, black bins mean
articles.summary: The Indian government has implemented a new four-bin waste segregation system, mandating separate bins for wet, dry, sanitary, and hazardous waste. This replaces the earlier two-bin model and aims to improve waste management nationwide. The rule applies to all households and establishments, with enforcement expected to reduce pollution and promote recycling. The government has directed states and municipalities to ensure compliance with the new system.

articles.title: Fact check: How much oil does US import via Strait of Hormuz? Trump's ‘almost no oil’ claim debunked
articles.summary: US Energy Information Administration (EIA) data shows that the US imports 0.5 million barrels of crude oil per day via the Strait of Hormuz, accounting for 7% of its crude imports. This contradicts former President Trump's claim that the US imports 'almost no oil' through this route. The Strait of Hormuz is a critical chokepoint for global oil trade, and disruptions here could impact global oil prices and supply chains, indirectly affecting India's energy security and import costs.
articles.raw_summary: EIA data shows US imports 0.5 million barrels of crude oil per day via Strait of Hormuz: 7% of crude imports, contradicting Trump’s claim of ‘almost no oil.’

articles.title: CBI Court Sentences Six Accused to Two Years’ Imprisonment with fine in Wildlife Smuggling Case involving Tiger and Leopard Body Parts
articles.summary: A CBI court has sentenced six accused to two years' imprisonment with a fine in a wildlife smuggling case involving tiger and leopard body parts. The tribunal directed the Assessing Officer to verify and recompute the taxable income. This ruling highlights the importance of correcting computational errors in tax cases.
articles.raw_summary: The tribunal found that STCG may have been counted twice, inflating taxable income. It directed verification and recomputation by the Assessing Officer. The ruling highlights correction of computational errors. The post Clerical Error Can’t Inflate Tax-ITAT Orders Rectification of Double STCG Addition appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: India’s VDA Tax Framework & CA Professional Obligation (Tax-Efficient Crypto Derivatives)
articles.summary: The article explains how taxation differs between spot crypto and derivatives. It highlights that classification determines tax rate and availability of deductions. The key takeaway is that correct classification can significantly reduce tax liability. The post India’s VDA Tax Framework & CA Professional Obligation (Tax-Efficient Crypto Derivatives) appeared first on TaxGuru .

This calendar lists major statutory deadlines across various laws and helps businesses avoid penalties through timely compliance. articles.title: U/s 153C Additions on Alleged On-Money Deleted – No Corroboration & No Cross-Examination
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) deleted additions made to an individual's income based on alleged cash payments. The tribunal held that there was no independent evidence or opportunity for cross-examination. This decision affects tax assessments and the use of third-party data in tax proceedings.
articles.raw_summary: The issue involved additions for alleged cash payments based on third-party data and statements. ITAT deleted the additions, holding that no independent evidence or cross-examination opportunity was provided. The post U/s 153C Additions on Alleged On-Money Deleted   No Corroboration   No Cross-Examination appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Stock Derivatives Are Not ‘Shares’: ITAT Delhi Rules on India-Mauritius Tax Treaty
articles.summary: The ITAT clarified that Article 13(3A) applies strictly to shares and not to derivative instruments. It held that derivative gains fall under residual provisions and are taxable in the resident country. The ruling emphasizes correct interpretation of DTAA provisions. The post Stock Derivatives Are Not ‘Shares’: ITAT Delhi Rules on India-Mauritius Tax Treaty appeared first on TaxGuru .
articles.raw_summary: The ITAT clarified that Article 13(3A) applies strictly to shares and not to derivative instruments. articles.title: ITAT Deletes Addition as Income Was Disclosed Despite Wrong Schedule Entry
articles.summary: The ITAT (Income Tax Appellate Tribunal) has ruled that reporting income in the wrong schedule does not amount to concealment. This means that tax cannot be levied when income is already disclosed, even if incorrectly classified. The ruling has implications for taxpayers who may have made errors in their tax returns.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that reporting income in the wrong schedule does not amount to concealment. It ruled that tax cannot be levied when income is already disclosed, even if incorrectly classified. The post ITAT Deletes Addition as Income Was Disclosed Despite Wrong Schedule Entry appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Allotment Letter = Agreement-ITAT Grants 56(2)(x) Relief, Limits Addition to 50% Share
articles.summary: The ITAT granted relief under section 56(2)(x) of the Income Tax Act, limiting the addition to 50% of the share. The tribunal recognized the allotment agreement and joint ownership, directing proportionate taxation and correct valuation basis.
articles.raw_summary: The tribunal set aside excessive addition by recognizing both the allotment agreement and joint ownership. It directed proportionate taxation and correct valuation basis. The ruling promotes fairness in assessments. The post Allotment Letter = Agreement-ITAT Grants 56(2)(x) Relief, Limits Addition to 50% Share appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Telangana relied on RBI borrowings for most of FY25: CAG report
articles.summary: Telangana's finances in 2024-25 saw extensive use of RBI's Special Drawing Facility and Ways and Means Advances. The state government borrowed Rs 27,730 crore via SDF and Rs 64,188 crore through WMA. Overdrafts totaling Rs 37,457 crore were also availed. This reliance on short-term borrowing mechanisms highlights financial management challenges. The CAG report detailed these transactions.

While AI accelerates content production and targeted outreach, its direct electoral impact is still considered limited, with human intelligence and traditional methods remaining crucial for strategy.

articles.title: Draft IT rule tweaks set off alarms over state powers and costs
articles.summary: Proposed amendments to India’s Information Technology Rules, 2021 spell an expanded compliance burden for intermediaries and a significant widening of government oversight of online content, said industry executives and experts.
articles.raw_summary: Proposed amendments to India’s Information Technology Rules, 2021 spell an expanded compliance burden for intermediaries and a significant widening of government oversight of online content, said industry executives and experts.

articles.title: Cyber resilience turns a boardroom priority: Rubrik CTO Arvind Nithrakashyap
articles.summary: Chennai-born Arvind Nithrakashyap believes Palo Alto-headquartered Rubrik is in the right spot to serve some of India’s cybersecurity needs at a time of rapid digitisation. This means a lot of investments need to go into securing those networks and data sets, he noted.

articles.title: Sunflower oil consumption set to wilt by 10% amid geopolitical heat, says Crisil
articles.summary: Indian refined sunflower oil demand is expected to fall about 10% in fiscal 2027 due to supply-chain disruptions from the Middle East conflict and higher prices, says Crisil. Consumers may switch to cheaper substitutes like rice bran and soybean oils, though overall revenues are likely to stay flat as higher prices offset the volume drop.

articles.title: Iran War: Fertiliser, agri-input stress emerges; Kharif outlook hinges on policy, farmer choices
articles.summary: India relies heavily on fertiliser imports routed through the Strait of Hormuz, which is currently facing disruptions.
articles.raw_summary: India relies heavily on fertiliser imports routed through the Strait of Hormuz, which is currently facing disruptions.

articles.title: India's economy puts up brave front as GST, auto sales and UPI payments surge despite Gulf war
articles.summary: India's economy shows remarkable resilience, with strong March figures in GST collections, vehicle sales, and digital payments, defying disruptions from the Gulf war. Despite a robust fiscal year-end, economists caution that rising costs and potential supply issues could soon impact the economy more visibly.
articles.raw_summary: India's economy shows remarkable resilience, with strong March figures in GST collections, vehicle sales, and digital payments, defying disruptions from the Gulf war. Despite a robust fiscal year-end, economists caution that rising costs and potential supply issues could soon impact the economy more visibly.

articles.title: India has managed war disruptions better than many other economies: P D Singh
articles.summary: Indian businesses and banks are navigating the West Asia conflict with resilience. Deal pipelines remain largely unaffected, with only war-linked transactions paused. While supply chain disruptions pose a risk, India's economic buffers are strong. Business activity in Gujarat's GIFT City is growing, and deposit mobilization is a key challenge for banks. The regulatory focus is on curbing mis-selling.

The regulatory focus is on curbing mis-selling.

articles.title: USTR lists India's UPI and data rules as trade barriers
articles.summary: America has identified several trade barriers in India. These include India's preference for local satellites for DTH services and the exclusion of US payment firms from UPI. Data localization rules and high tariffs on agriculture products are also cited. The US is pushing for more access to India's dairy market. Discussions for an interim trade agreement are ongoing.

Data localization rules and high tariffs on agriculture products are also cited. The US is pushing for more access to India's dairy market. Discussions for an interim trade agreement are ongoing.

articles.title: SEZ units can sell locally at lower duty for one year
articles.summary: India has announced a one-time relief for Special Economic Zone (SEZ) units, allowing them to sell goods in the domestic market at a lower customs duty for one year. This measure, along with a six-month extension of fiscal benefits under RoDTEP, aims to support exporters amid global crises and enhance export competitiveness by enabling better capacity utilization and liquidity.

This measure, along with a six-month extension of fiscal benefits under RoDTEP, aims to support exporters amid global crises and enhance export competitiveness by enabling better capacity utilization and liquidity.

articles.title: Despite rate reset, GST collection rises on strong consumption
articles.summary: India's Goods and Services Tax collections ended the fiscal year strongly. March collections surpassed two lakh crore rupees for the third time. This indicates robust consumption despite rate reductions. Import-linked revenues drove much of this growth. Experts remain cautious for the coming months due to global turmoil. Rising crude oil prices pose a risk.

Rising crude oil prices pose a risk.

articles.title: India permits exports of essential commodities to Maldives for 2026-27
articles.summary: India will export essential goods to Maldives for 2026-27. This includes eggs, rice, sugar, and construction materials like sand and stone aggregate. These exports are part of a bilateral trade agreement. India's commitment to supporting Maldives' development is highlighted. The agreement ensures supply of crucial items for Maldives' growing construction sector.

articles.title: India has adequate seed stock available amid the Israel-Iran war, says govt official
articles.summary: India assures ample seed availability for the upcoming sowing season. This comes as global supply chains face disruptions. The nation boasts a self-reliant seed system. For the Kharif Season 2026, a surplus of 19.29 lakh quintals is reported. This ensures agricultural needs are met, providing stability for farmers.

articles.title: Amaravati bill to restore investor confidence, unlock Rs 56,000-crore for development: Pemmasani
articles.summary: The Lok Sabha has passed a bill making Amaravati the sole capital of Andhra Pradesh. This move is set to revive investor confidence and unlock significant infrastructure development. The bill provides legal clarity, ending years of uncertainty. Amaravati is envisioned as a global-standard city and a key growth engine for the state. This will accelerate investments and generate employment.

Amaravati is envisioned as a global-standard city and a key growth engine for the state. This will accelerate investments and generate employment.

articles.title: Dedicated rare earth corridors will reduce import dependence: Govt
articles.summary: India is establishing dedicated rare earth corridors in Odisha, Kerala, Andhra Pradesh, and Tamil Nadu to reduce import dependence and boost domestic capabilities in strategic sectors. These corridors will focus on mining, processing, research, and manufacturing, aligning with national goals of self-reliance and net-zero emissions. A joint working group is formulating guidelines to implement these initiatives.

A joint working group is formulating guidelines to implement these initiatives.

articles.title: Govt extends RoDTEP benefits for exporters till September 30
articles.summary: Indian exporters receive a six-month extension for RoDTEP scheme benefits until September 30. This move aims to counter trade disruptions from the West Asia crisis. The scheme offers refunds on duties and taxes for manufactured goods. This support helps exporters manage rising freight costs and trade risks.

The scheme offers refunds on duties and taxes for manufactured goods. This support helps exporters manage rising freight costs and trade risks.

articles.title: India grants temporary customs duty relief for SEZ goods sold domestically
articles.summary: India will offer temporary customs duty relief for goods produced in special economic zones and sold domestically. The reduced duty rates, ranging from 5% to 12.5%, will be effective from April 1, 2026, to March 31, 2027, benefiting businesses that commenced production by March 31, 2025.

articles.title: Courier export cap removed to boost ecomm shipments, cut logistics delays
articles.summary: Starting April 1, India has abolished the ₹10 lakh threshold on courier exports, a shift aimed at propelling e-commerce forward and supporting small enterprises. The introduction of a quick return mechanism for unclaimed parcels is also noteworthy, designed to alleviate shipping delays and enhance overall delivery efficiency for cross-border commerce.

articles.title: RBI's net short position rises to $77 billion in Feb as it defends rupee
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India's net short position in forwards rose to $77 billion in February. This move impacts the central bank's capacity to protect the rupee. The forward book is projected to exceed $100 billion. This situation affects foreign exchange reserves and rupee liquidity. The market now closely watches import cover levels.

articles.title: US tariffs drag India apparel export growth to modest 1.5%: ICRA
articles.summary: Indian apparel exports saw modest growth, impacted by US tariffs. A weaker rupee helped boost earnings in local currency. Shipments to the UK and UAE provided some relief. ICRA forecasts stronger revenue growth and improved financial health for exporters in the coming year. Geopolitical risks remain a concern for the sector.

articles.title: Services, remittances boost India's invisible inflows to $464 billion in FY26
articles.summary: Receipts from the services sector, which accounted for two-third of the total, climbed about 9% to $310 billion, data showed. These are known as invisible receipts as these reflect earnings a country generates from IT services, tourism and remittances   transfers rather than international trade in physical goods.

articles.title: 10 foreign ships with energy cargo for India stranded in Persian Gulf
articles.summary: Ten foreign-flagged ships carrying energy cargo are stranded in the Persian Gulf, with 18 Indian vessels also in the region, raising concerns over Strait of Hormuz transit. The government prioritizes Indian ships, with two LPG carriers having successfully passed. Insurance costs have significantly increased due to the heightened risk.

articles.title: 'India's economy will double every 7-8 years,' says Union Minister Gajendra Singh Shekhawat
articles.summary: India's economy is set for significant growth, aiming for 30 trillion dollars by 2047. However, ongoing global conflicts pose risks. These tensions could reduce India's economic expansion by one percent and increase inflation by 1.5 percent. The nation's reliance on imported energy makes it vulnerable to these disruptions. Despite these challenges, India's economy shows strong momentum.

articles.title: Iran war shock: Middle East conflict could cut 1 ppt from India’s FY27 GDP outlook
articles.summary: Iran War: A persistent Middle East conflict could reduce India's real GDP growth by 1 percentage point and increase retail inflation by 1.5 percentage points from baseline estimates. This impact stems from disruptions to global oil and energy markets, affecting employment-intensive sectors and aggregate demand. The government may need to implement countercyclical policies and augment the Economic Stabilization Fund.

articles.title: "Unfortunate for India," Ex R&AW Chief hints at probable inflation if West Asia situation escalates
articles.summary: Former R AW Chief Vikram Sood expressed concern over the West Asia conflict's impact on India, particularly potential oil and fertilizer shortages leading to inflation. He called the attack on Iran and the killing of its Supreme Leader unfortunate, emphasizing India's dependence on the Strait of Hormuz while noting Israel as an ally.

articles.title: India diesel exports jump 20% in March amid Iran war
articles.summary: India boosted diesel exports significantly in March. This surge was driven by favorable economics for middle distillates. Geopolitical events in West Asia tightened supply, increasing diesel and jet fuel prices. Refiners adjusted production to capitalize on these higher margins. India also increased domestic LPG production to compensate for lost imports. Overall refined product exports saw a slight decline.

articles.title: Pulses import duties likely to stay unchanged
articles.summary: New Delhi is set to maintain current import duties on pulses. The government will also extend the duty-free period for tur and urad. This move aims to prevent price increases. Officials anticipate an El Nino affecting monsoon rainfall. Production of tur and urad is expected to decline. This could lead to higher prices and food inflation.

articles.title: Licious’ FY26 revenues grow 47% to Rs 1,166 crore
articles.summary: Meat and seafood retailer Licious reported a 47% rise in revenues, at Rs 1,166 crore, for the financial year ended March 31, 2026, up from Rs 795 crore a year earlier. The company attributed its growth to strengthening its presence and improving delivery speed across key micro-markets in Bengaluru, Mumbai, and Delhi NCR. The company said it plans to expand to 10 micro-markets across these cities in FY27.

articles.title: Will crude oil prices tank? Someone has bet nearly $1 billion on this via leverage
articles.summary: Investors have placed nearly $1 billion in leveraged bets on a potential decline in crude oil prices. The United States Oil Fund (USO) and United States Brent Oil Fund (BNO) saw record inflows of $700 million and $600 million respectively in March, the highest since the pandemic. These movements reflect market sentiment and could impact India's energy costs and inflation, given the country's heavy reliance on oil imports.
articles.raw_summary: Its not just the bearish bets, even the bullish funds have drawn record money. The United States Oil Fund (USO) attracted flows worth nearly $700 million in March, the most since the pandemic, while the United States Brent Oil Fund (BNO) saw flows worth a record $600 million.
